title: cotoneaster buxifolius var. typicus
intro: Cotoneaster buxifolius var. typicus, also known as the dwarf box-leaved cotoneaster, is a type of flowering shrub native to China. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and combat infections. The plant's medicinal properties also contribute to various health benefits, including improving digestion, reducing fever, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Cotoneaster buxifolius var. typicus are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
